## Root Cause Summary

Issue: Applying a 0W power limit policy on the MemorySubsystem domain (D2) via NM Redfish schema has no effect. Power values remain at workload-max (~70W) instead of dropping to the floor/min capability value. This is a regression — D2 power limiting worked correctly in BKC 12.

Last Known Good: BKC 12

First Known Bad: BKC 14

Other domains (D0, D1, D5): Working as expected — not affected.

Reproduction Steps:

Run stress-ng workload (CPU + MEM, on X4 system)

Read MemorySubsystem (D2) power st

## Raw HSD Text

<!-- The Copilot agent extracts root cause, fix description, code refs, and diagrams. -->

### Description
Issue: Applying a 0W power limit policy on the MemorySubsystem domain (D2) via NM Redfish schema has no effect. Power values remain at workload-max (~70W) instead of dropping to the floor/min capability value. This is a regression — D2 power limiting worked correctly in BKC 12.

Last Known Good: BKC 12

First Known Bad: BKC 14

Other domains (D0, D1, D5): Working as expected — not affected.

Reproduction Steps:

Run stress-ng workload (CPU + MEM, on X4 system)

Read MemorySubsystem (D2) power statistics → ~70W observed

Create 0W policy for D2 domain using NM Redfish schema

Read D2 power → still ~70W (expected: drop to floor/min capability)

Delete the 0W policy

Read D2 power → still ~70W (expected: uncapped but at baseline min)

Expected
: Power should drop to floor value when 0W policy is active; after deletion, should reflect uncapped minimum.

Actual: Power remains at ~70W throughout — NM policy has zero effect on D2.
